content: IT Component
Atechnical building block of an IT product, independent of business logic, sourced from vendors or developed in-house.
Enables organizations to run, maintain, and change IT products.
Used within technical platforms or directly in IT products.
Lifecycle planning, transparency, responsibility mapping, redundancy/gap analysis, synergy identification, operational reliability.
Enterprise Architects, Business Analysts, Business Units
Supports strategic IT planning and architecture governance.
Ensure completeness and accuracy to support planning and operations.

description: Arelational database platform used to store and manage structured data.
Adistributed streaming platform for real-time data pipelines and messaging.
Asecure and scalable server operating system.
Ahigh-performance web server and reverse proxy.
Provides the libraries and components to run Java applications.
Asoftware framework for building and running Windows applications.
Amonitoring and alerting toolkit for IT infrastructure.
Identity and access management service for cloud and on- premises environments.
Aplatform for developing, shipping, and running containerized applications.
Automation server for continuous integration and delivery.
Aproprietary logging library developed for internal use.
Amanaged service for creating, publishing, and securing APIs.

content: Examples of IT Components
Informative / Reference
To provide categorized, real-world examples of IT components for better understanding and documentation.
Enterprise Architects, Business Analysts, Business Stakeholders
Supplementary material to the definition and purpose of IT components in enterprise architecture documentation.
Explains the rationale for listing examples, emphasizes practical relevance, and highlights the role of IT components in running, maintaining, and changing IT products.
